mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a funds rate cut, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 8.5%, consumer spending power collapses and recession probabilities rise, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a t-test against baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during post-shock recovery periods.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of observations within the specified regime, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation crossing above 0.4% forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered months, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, which supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal generates negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy variables.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of observations within the late-cycle regime, or the GDP growth coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in regime-split regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in industrial production crossing below -1.5% indicates severe supply-side contraction and impending earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered quarters, or the signal fails to achieve a positive Sharpe ratio in a backtested trading strategy.
